Title: Inactivation of GH3.5 by COP1-mediated K63-linked ubiquitination promotes seedling hypocotyl elongation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts IAA-Asp to oxIAA-Asp, contributing to light-associated accumulation of oxidized IAA conjugates
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced pathway logic and metabolite measurements of oxIAA-Asp; no direct genetic test in this paper
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a main experimental focus, but directly needed to interpret oxIAA-Asp accumulation from IAA-Asp in the Results/Discussion
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAO1 is invoked as the enzyme converting IAA-Asp to oxIAA-Asp. 2. Light increased oxIAA-Asp accumulation, consistent with active downstream oxidation of IAA-Asp. 3. DAO1 itself was not directly manipulated in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible downstream auxin-catabolism target, though not functionally tested here

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family, group II acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter for GH3.5-GFP overexpression; 35S promoter in transient ubiquitin-expression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin inactivation/homeostasis; accumulation of IAA-amino acid conjugates is associated with inhibition of hypocotyl elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA amino acid conjugation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates indole-3-acetic acid (IAA) to amino acids, especially Asp, and also contributes to Glu conjugation, producing IAA-amino acid conjugates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA-amino acid conjugate accumulation and inhibits hypocotyl elongation; its activity is inhibited by COP1-mediated K63 ubiquitination in the dark
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic analysis of gh3.5-1 and cop1-4 gh3.5-1; nalacin treatment; in vitro pull-down; LCI; Co-IP; BiFC; in vitro ubiquitination assay; in vivo ubiquitination assay; enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; metabolite profiling by UHPLC/UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for light/COP1-regulated IAA conjugation, but classical rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression is higher in darkness than continuous white light; induced by auxin; decreased in cop1-4 in the dark
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in dark-grown seedlings; gradually decreases upon transfer from dark to light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness versus continuous white light; exogenous IAA; nalacin; 2,4-D
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher GH3.5 activity is associated with shorter hypocotyls and reduced elongation growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COP1 does not alter GH3.5 protein stability but suppresses catalytic activity through K63-linked ubiquitination; kinetic data suggest near-noncompetitive inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.5 is the principal group II GH3 member genetically required for the short-hypocotyl phenotype of cop1-4 in darkness. 2. GH3.5 directly interacts with COP1 and is modified mainly by COP1-dependent K63-linked ubiquitination rather than degradation-linked K48 ubiquitination. 3. K63 ubiquitination strongly inhibits GH3.5 enzymatic activity, reducing conversion of IAA to IAA-Asp/IAA-Glu in the dark. 4. Light suppresses COP1-dependent K63 ubiquitination, thereby activating GH3.5-dependent IAA conjugation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune auxin metabolism and seedling architecture by editing ubiquitination control or GH3 activity; caution that enhanced GH3 activity may reduce elongation growth and cause pleiotropic growth effects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS substrate screening; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family, group II acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA amino acid conjugation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates IAA with amino acids as a group II GH3 family member
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to IAA conjugate production, but alone does not significantly mediate COP1-controlled hypocotyl elongation in the tested double mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS candidate identification; genetic analysis with cop1-4; higher-order mutant metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely similar growth restraint when active, but not individually resolved here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a candidate substrate in screening, but genetic tests indicated GH3.5 rather than GH3.6 is the major COP1-linked effector in dark hypocotyl control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.6 was identified in the COP1/K63-ubiquitination substrate screen. 2. gh3.6 did not rescue the short-hypocotyl phenotype of cop1-4 by itself. 3. Together with GH3.5/GH3.9/GH3.17, it contributes redundantly to excess IAA-conjugate accumulation in cop1 mutants and to light-responsive IAA metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in multigene stacking/editing of group II GH3 family for auxin-homeostasis engineering rather than as a single major target
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS substrate screening; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family, group II acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA amino acid conjugation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Redundantly conjugates IAA to amino acids as a group II GH3 member
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to elevated IAA-conjugate accumulation and short hypocotyl phenotype in cop1 background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to growth restraint via auxin inactivation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied only as part of higher-order group II GH3 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.9 functions redundantly with GH3.5/GH3.6/GH3.17 in COP1-regulated IAA conjugation. 2. Removing GH3.9 together with other group II GH3s lowers IAA-Asp/IAA-Glu accumulation in cop1 mutants. 3. It contributes to light-responsive IAA-Asp plus oxIAA-Asp accumulation at family level.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for multiplex editing of group II GH3 genes to modulate auxin conjugation capacity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 family, group II acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Group II GH3-mediated IAA amino acid conjugation branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Redundantly conjugates IAA to amino acids as a group II GH3 member
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes redundantly to COP1-opposed IAA conjugation and short hypocotyl phenotype in cop1 background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to growth restraint via auxin inactivation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as a redundant family member in quadruple mutant backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.17 contributes redundantly with GH3.5/GH3.6/GH3.9 to COP1-regulated IAA conjugation. 2. Loss of GH3.17 together with the other three GH3 genes lowers excess IAA-conjugate accumulation in cop1-4. 3. Family-level GH3 activity is required for full light-promoted IAA-Asp plus oxIAA-Asp accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for multiplex engineering of group II GH3 genes controlling auxin metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: cop1-4; cop1-6; YFP-COP1/cop1-6; CRISPR cop1-4-like allele
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase with RING, coiled-coil, and WD40 repeat domains
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin inactivation/homeostasis associated with modulation of hypocotyl elongation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin metabolism via inhibition of group II GH3-mediated IAA conjugation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inhibits GH3.5 enzymatic activity through K63-linked polyubiquitination without promoting degradation; antagonizes group II GH3 function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hypocotyl elongation in the dark by suppressing conversion of IAA to IAA-amino acid conjugates
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cop1 mutant phenotyping; genetic interaction with gh3 mutants; IP-MS; pull-down; LCI; Co-IP; BiFC; in vitro and in vivo ubiquitination assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major dark-active suppressor of IAA conjugate production via direct inhibition of GH3.5; required for high GH3.5 K63 ubiquitination in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: COP1-SPA4 complex used as active E3 in vitro; COP1-GH3.5 interaction validated
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light suppresses COP1 function; darkness maintains COP1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functionally active in dark; suppressed by light
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of COP1 causes short hypocotyl/constitutive photomorphogenic phenotypes and overaccumulation of IAA conjugates
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike classic COP1 substrates, GH3.5 is not destabilized; study establishes a non-proteolytic COP1 function in photomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COP1 is required for substantial K63-linked ubiquitination accumulation in dark-grown seedlings. 2. COP1 directly interacts with GH3.5 in the nucleus and catalyzes mainly K63-linked ubiquitination of GH3.5. 3. This modification suppresses GH3.5 activity rather than its stability, reducing IAA-amino acid conjugation. 4. Light relieves COP1 inhibition, increasing GH3-dependent IAA conjugate accumulation and restricting hypocotyl elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering seedling architecture and auxin metabolic outputs, but deployment requires caution because COP1 broadly controls photomorphogenesis and many pleiotropic traits
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS substrate screening; no public accession reported
